text.title: Positioning Diff Log Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that hollow out a positioning diff log. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Live-page edit without diff entry — founder edits the landing page directly, the canonical POSITIONING.md and the diff log drift out of sync.
failure-mode.detector: positioning.diff.md head entry text differs from live landing page hero/sub-hero text
failure-mode.repair: Pre-deploy hook runs scripts/positioning-vs-landing-diff.py and blocks the deploy if there is drift. Backfill missing entries with the trigger marked "founder-insight" and a verbatim quote from the founder's reasoning (even a short Slack message counts).

failure-mode.description: Synthetic source quotes — founder paraphrases customer conversations from memory instead of pulling verbatim, the log loses anchoring power.
failure-mode.detector: source_quotes lacks source URLs / IDs OR quotes are not retrievable in the original system (support tool, call recording, competitor archive)
failure-mode.repair: Require source linkability: every quote includes a URL or ID that resolves to the original artifact. Quotes that cannot be linked are marked "reconstructed" so future readers can downweight them.

failure-mode.description: 30/90-day checks never populated — founder publishes the entry, never returns to check, decision quality stays uninformed.
failure-mode.detector: more than 30% of entries have a 30d check that is > 14 days past due AND still pending
failure-mode.repair: Calendar reminders per entry on the due date. Block new entries when > 2 previous entries are past-due on their 30-day check. Monthly review fills in checks or explicitly marks them inconclusive (with reason).

failure-mode.description: Trigger-bucket dominance — 80% of entries are tagged founder-insight, meaning the founder is iterating without external signal and using the bucket as a catch-all.
failure-mode.detector: one trigger type accounts for > 70% of entries over the trailing 6 months
failure-mode.repair: Force re-tagging of recent entries: was there really no customer-call, support-pattern, or competitor-move that prompted the change? If genuinely yes, the founder is iterating without data — pause new entries until external signals are collected.

failure-mode.description: Iteration without rollback — founder ships 8 entries in 4 months, none ever rolled back, no entry actually moved the metric.
failure-mode.detector: over 6 months: 5+ entries shipped, 0 marked rolled-back, monthly_review.rollbacks_recommended consistently empty AND check verdicts skew not-observed
failure-mode.repair: Apply the rollback discipline: any entry whose 90d check is not-observed becomes a rollback candidate. Founder picks either a true rollback to the prior positioning OR a documented "we will live with this not-observed result because of X" note.

failure-mode.description: Microcopy creep — entries proliferate for button copy, microcopy, FAQ tweaks, drowning the signal of real positioning changes.
failure-mode.detector: median entry length < 30 words OR entries describe changes below sub-hero level
failure-mode.repair: Move microcopy changes to a separate copy-changelog. Reserve positioning-diff-log for hero, sub-hero, audience descriptor, and value-prop changes. Quarterly cleanup PR re-categorises misclassified entries.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: A founder's pressure to "just edit the landing page real quick" must NOT bypass the diff-entry requirement. The discipline exists precisely because the cost of skipping the entry feels small in the moment and the cost of having skipped it compounds over months.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Quarterly: founder reads through the trailing 90 days of entries, computes the hit-rate per trigger type, and updates their own iteration heuristics. The log is not the goal; the goal is calibrating which kinds of positioning iterations actually move the metric.
